title: Target Tractability Schema
description: Schema for assessing the tractability of a target for small molecule and antibody drug development, based on various biological and chemical features.

uniprot_acc.description: The UniProt accession number for the protein target.

sm_precedence.description: Indicates the highest level of small molecule chemical precedence for the target, e.g., 'Clinical', 'Preclinical', 'Tool', 'Known Ligand', 'No Ligand'.

sm_domain_annotation.description: A summary of relevant small molecule druggable domain annotations for the target, such as 'Kinase', 'GPCR', 'Ion Channel', 'Nuclear Receptor'.

sm_pharmaprojects.description: Boolean indicating if the target has been investigated by small molecules in PharmaProjects, suggesting active drug development.

sm_chembl.description: Boolean indicating if the target has associated small molecule activity data in ChEMBL, a database of chemical biology data.

sm_gostar.description: Boolean indicating if the target has associated small molecule data in GOSTAR, another comprehensive medicinal chemistry database.

sm_pdb.description: Boolean indicating if the target has 3D structural data (e.g., protein-ligand complex) available in the Protein Data Bank (PDB), aiding small molecule design.

sm_cxg.description: Boolean indicating if the target is found in the Chemogenomics database (CXG).

sm_rf_proteomics.description: Boolean indicating if the target has evidence of small molecule binding from reactive fragment screens.

sm_eta.description: Small molecule experimental Tractability Assessment (ETA) score or categorization, providing an overall assessment of small molecule druggability.

ab_pharmaprojects.description: Boolean indicating if the target has been investigated by antibodies in PharmaProjects, suggesting active antibody drug development.

secreted.description: Boolean indicating if the protein target is secreted, making it potentially accessible to antibody therapies.

extracellular_region.description: Boolean indicating if a significant part of the protein target is located in the extracellular region, which is crucial for antibody binding.
